results.title: One for the road
results.creator_credit: Dennis A. Hill and Australian Commonwealth Film Unit
results.brief_description: A few hours in the life of Bill Smith show why you should not mix drinking with driving. The affect of alcohol is illustrated with a model brain: although not drunk Smith’s brain power was sufficiently impaired to cause a fatal accident.

results.title: Nullarbor hideout
results.creator_credit: Tim Burstall, John Martin-Jones, Australian Children's Television Foundation and Australian Commonwealth Film Unit
results.brief_description: The adventures of a group of children living at Cook on the Nullarbor Plain. They discover a swimming pool in a limestone cave and make the cave their hide-out. The children become involved with kangaroo shooters who are wanted by police for car stealing. Cast includes Tom Burstall, Betty Burstall, David Crocker, Frances Hatham, Marcus Skipper.

results.title: Not to lose you my language: bilingual education in the Northern Territory = Wangka walytjalampa titu ngaranytjaku
results.creator_credit: Greg Reading, Malcolm Otton, Timothy Read and Film Australia
results.brief_description: Gives an impression of the day-to-day operation of the Aboriginal bilingual program in three widely separated schools in the Northern Territory: Milingimbi, Yuendumu and the outstation of Yayayi.

results.title: No strangers here
results.creator_credit: K Sternberg, Australian National Film Board and Dept of Immigration
results.brief_description: Dealing with the problem of assimilation of migrants, the film shows how newcomers to Australia are finding new friends and happiness in the land of their adoption through the co-operation and sympathetic understanding of ordinary citizens. They begin their new life in a country town and through the personal interest of the editor of the newspaper, the newcomers become part of the life of the community.

results.title: New decimal coin designs
results.brief_description: Designs for Australia’s six new decimal coins prepared by local designer Stuart Devlin, are presented by the Federal Treasurer, Mr. Harold Holt, and a member of the advisory panel on design Mr. Hal Missingham, who worked closely with the designer and helped solve technical problems. The coins feature seven of Australia’s unique fauna- the spiny anteater, lyre-bird, frilled lizard, feather-tailed glider, platypus, kangaroo and emu.

results.title: Music camp
results.creator_credit: R. Maslyn Williams and Australian National Film Board
results.brief_description: Music students are able to attend a summer camp at Point Lonsdale, Victoria, for three weeks every year. This camp is organised by the National Fitness Council, the Founder being John Bishop. Students attend tutorial classes each morning in the open air and the afternoons are free to swim, fish, sunbathe and practice. In the evenings students usually give a concert for friends and visitors to the camp.

results.title: Moving off on hills, reversing and parking
results.creator_credit: John Edwards, Frank Bagnall and Australian Commonwealth Film Unit
results.brief_description:
Episode number 5 of Series “How to Drive”.
Having gained experience in the fundamentals of car control, the learner is now ready to acquire the skills required to complete those driving manoeuvres in which co-ordination of control movements plays an important part: the hill start, reversing and parking.

results.title: Millions for the finding
results.creator_credit: Hugh Alexander, Eric Thompson and Australian National Film Board
results.brief_description: Survey of Australia’s mineral resources. Shows silver-lead-zinc mines at Broken Hill; copper-silver-lead-zinc mines at Mt Lyell in Tasmania; coal from the various mines of New South Wales; iron ore from South Australia and Yampi Sound to the blast furnaces at Newcastle, Port kembla and Whyalla; scheelite, from King Island in Bass Strait; oil from Exmouth Gulf, and radio-active minerals from Radium Hill. Modern methods of mineral detection are shown, including aerial surveying.

results.title: Mike and Stefani
results.creator_credit: R. Maslyn Williams and Australian Commonwealth Film Unit
results.brief_description: Mike and Stefani, a young Ukrainian couple, spent World War II in German labour camps. After the war the couple were reunited and as two of the eight million “displaced persons” in European refugee camps they sought to resettle in Australia. The film had a twofold purpose, to counter the criticism within Australia that post-war immigration procedures for such people were inadequate and that some “undesirables” were slipping through the net, and to encourage Australians to accept the sudden influx of non-British immigrants in the immediate post-war years. Photographer: R.G. Pearse.
